server.title: Server
server.allowDefaultCredentialsWarning: Warning: allowing default credentials is insecure

database.title: Databases
database.noDatabasesGenuine: No databases exist yet. Create one via Restore or from a running server.
database.pgUnreachable: PostgreSQL for v{{version}} not reachable on port {{port}} — is the container running? A deviating DB_PORT in the version's .env or an active migration group can also redirect this port.
database.dropWarning: This action cannot be undone. All data will be permanently lost.
database.typeToConfirm: Type the database name to confirm
database.purgeMasterDataWarning: WARNING: This DELETEs customers/vendors, CRM/HR data, messages, attachments. Keeps products, pricelists, users, companies, config.
database.restoreDescription: Restore a backup into a new or existing database (v{{version}})
database.dryRunPassed: Dry run passed — ready to restore

restoreHelp.drop: Deletes an existing database with the same name first — unchecked, the restore aborts if the name already exists.
restoreHelp.sanitize: Enables all five cleanup steps below at once — including Purge master data!
restoreHelp.deactivateCron: Disables cron jobs and outgoing mail servers after the restore, so the copy never sends real e-mails.
restoreHelp.neutralize: Runs Odoo's native neutralize (mail, payment providers, bank sync off). Needs a working venv for this version.
restoreHelp.anonymize: Replaces personal data with Faker values (GDPR-safe test data).
restoreHelp.wipe: Blanks message and attachment content (mail_message, ir_attachment).
restoreHelp.purgeMasterData: Template-DB reset: deletes customers/vendors, CRM/HR data, messages and attachments; keeps products, pricelists, users, companies and configuration.
restoreHelp.purgeTransactions: Deletes all movement data (stock, sales, purchase, accounting, MRP, POS) and zeroes stock. Not part of Sanitize.
restoreHelp.anonymizeUsers: Anonymizes user logins and sets the dev password below on all users.
restoreHelp.recompute: Recomputes stored computed fields so overviews show the anonymized data. Runs only together with Anonymize (enabled automatically when Anonymize runs).
restoreHelp.uninstallModules: Comma-separated technical module names, uninstalled before the cleanup steps run.

docker.title: Docker / Containers
docker.appleContainerDesc: Native macOS Linux containers on Apple Silicon
docker.noRuntimeDesc: Neither Docker nor Apple Container found in PATH
docker.serviceDownApple: The Apple Container service (apiserver) is not running.
docker.serviceDownDocker: The Docker daemon is not running.

venv.title: Virtual Environments
venv.description: Manage Python virtual environments for each Odoo version. UV creates and installs requirements.txt automatically.
venv.removeConfirmText: This will delete the virtual environment directory and all installed packages. The venv can be recreated with "Setup" at any time.

repos.title: Repositories
repos.description: Clone/update Git repositories and regenerate Odoo configuration from repos.yaml. "Pull" updates existing repos without cloning.

env.title: Environment Files
env.description: Manage .env files for each Odoo version. Contains ports, PostgreSQL credentials, and platform configuration.

playbook.title: Playbook Runner
playbook.description: Execute YAML playbooks or build inline step sequences. NDJSON events stream live per step.
playbook.serverStepsHint: Server-mode steps (backup/restore etc.) require a saved playbook with a targets: block — they are not available as inline steps.
playbook.legacyStreamingHint: Live per-step progress requires odoodev ≥ 0.51 — your installed version delivers all step results when the playbook finishes.

details.title: Playbook Details
details.serverModeWarning: This playbook contains server-mode steps (container/database operations).
details.serverModeWarningTargets: This playbook contains server-mode steps targeting: {{targets}}.

init.title: Init Wizard
init.description: Initialize Odoo development environments or configure odoodev settings.
init.skipReposDesc: Don't run git clone — use existing repos or clone later
init.skipDockerDesc: Don't start PostgreSQL container — start it manually later
init.resetToDefaultsDesc: Overwrite existing config with default values
init.configPath: Creates/updates ~/.config/odoodev/config.yaml with base directory, active versions, and database credentials.

migrate.title: Migration Groups
migrate.description: Share PostgreSQL container + filestore between two Odoo versions during database migration. Each version keeps its own venv, repos, and config.
migrate.sourceTargetMustDiffer: Source and target must be different versions.
migrate.removeConfirmText: This removes the migration group definition. Does not stop any containers or delete data.
migrate.experimentalHint: This panel reads migrate list/status from the CLI's text output — display may break when the CLI format changes. Verify results in the terminal for critical migrations.

doctor.title: Health Check
doctor.description: Checks prerequisites (uv, Docker/Apple Container, PostgreSQL, Node.js, system libs) and version-specific PostgreSQL port + venv packages.
doctor.noResults: No results yet. Click "Re-run" to start.

install.odoodevNotInstalledText: odoodev is the CLI tool that this GUI controls. It will be installed via uv (Python package manager).
install.uvNotInstalledText: uv is the Python package manager needed to install and update odoodev.

editor.title: Editor
editor.description: Edit odoodev configuration, per-version config files and playbooks.
editor.newPlaybookDescription: Creates a YAML playbook from a minimal template.
editor.nameInvalidChars: The name must not contain path separators.
editor.nameDuplicate: A playbook with this name already exists.
editor.unsavedMessage: The current file has unsaved changes. Save them before switching?
editor.noFileSelected: Select a file on the left to edit it.
editor.cliUpdateHint: The installed odoodev CLI does not support 'config paths' yet — showing a reduced file list. Update odoodev (Settings) to edit template and generated odoo.conf files.

choice.select_filename_ts: Newest by timestamp in the filename (filename_timestamp)

common.name: Playbook name:
common.on_error: What should happen when a step fails?

create.env_exists: env_file already exists at {path} — pass --force to overwrite or set env_file.generate to false

steps.question: Which steps should the playbook run?

lang.persist: Save as default language for odoodev?
lang.question: Sprache / Language?

secrets.detected: These environment variables are referenced in your playbook:
secrets.exists_merge: {path} already exists — merge new keys into it (existing values kept)?
secrets.generate: Generate a secrets .env file for this playbook?
secrets.none: No secret values entered — no env file written.
secrets.skipped: No secrets file written — create {path} manually (chmod 600) before running from cron.

dest.header: Destination — the container pair the backup is restored INTO
dest.name: Destination name (identifier in the playbook):
dest.self_mirror_confirm: Really restore back onto the source system?
dest.self_mirror_warning: Destination uses the SAME database container as the source ('{name}') — the restore would overwrite the system you just backed up!

server.intro:
A mirror copies one Odoo system onto another: SOURCE -> DESTINATION.
The assistant asks three things: 1) Where does the data come from?
2) Where should it go? 3) What should happen along the way?

recipe.question: Which optional steps should the mirror include? (restore is always part of it)
recipe.rebuild_hint: The release access code lives in release.txt inside the build folder; the container must be an active entry in docker2update.yaml.
recipe.update_all_restart: Restart the container after the module update?

flag.anonymize: anonymize — replace personal data with fake data
flag.deactivate_cron: deactivate_cron — disable cron jobs & outgoing mail
flag.neutralize: neutralize — neutralize Odoo (payments, IAP, webhooks, bank sync off)
flag.purge_transactions: purge_transactions — delete transactional data (orders, postings, stock)

restore.neutralize_needs_start: neutralize needs the Odoo container running — without 'start after restore' only the psql part runs; the odoo-bin neutralize step is skipped.
restore.purge_master_data: Also purge ALL master data (destructive template reset)?
restore.purge_warning: purge_master_data deletes partners, CRM/HR content and attachments — only for template databases!
restore.sanitize: What should happen to the restored database?

rpc.args: Positional args as JSON list (e.g. ["key", "value"], empty = none):
rpc.hint: Credentials come from ODOO_USER/ODOO_PASSWORD in the env_file.
rpc.kwargs: Keyword args as JSON object (empty = none):

source.auto_handoff: The restore will automatically use the backup file this run creates — nothing to configure.
source.fresh: Create a fresh backup from the running source system
source.header: Source — the container pair the backup is taken FROM
source.name: Source name (identifier in the playbook):
source.newest: Use the newest backup file from a directory
source.question: What is the SOURCE of the mirror?

sql.none_added: No statements added — skipping the SQL step.

target.data_dir: Host data dir (empty = resolve via docker inspect):
target.name: Target name:
target.need_one: At least one target is required.

step.flow_sub: What should happen along the way?
step.source_sub: Where does the data come from?

type.question: Which kind of playbook do you want to create?

wizard.subtitle: Answer the questions — a runnable playbook YAML comes out.

gui.title: Playbook Assistant
gui.subtitle: Answer the questions — a runnable playbook YAML comes out.
gui.loadFailed: Could not load the wizard schema (odoodev >= 0.55 required)
gui.derivedPattern: Backup file pattern is derived from the source target automatically.
gui.noTargets: Define at least one target first
